title: Salmorejo
subtitle: Thick cold Spanish tomato and bread soup
excerpt: Salmorejo is a thick, cold Spanish soup from Córdoba, made from blended tomatoes, stale bread, garlic, olive oil, and vinegar. It is denser and richer than gazpacho, served with a topping of hard-boiled egg and ham. It keeps well in the fridge for two days.
sourceNotes: Salmorejo is a classic of Córdoban cooking in Andalusia, closely related to gazpacho but thicker and richer. The bread-to-tomato ratio is higher and the result is almost a cold cream. It is traditionally finished with torn serrano ham and hard-boiled egg.

recipe.batchNotes: Keeps in the fridge for 2 days. Stir before serving as it can separate slightly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best made the day before; the flavour deepens overnight.

content.text: Salmorejo is a cold, thick soup of blended tomatoes and stale bread, enriched with olive oil and sharpened with vinegar. It is denser than gazpacho and more filling, served as a starter in a bowl with ham and egg on top. The key ratio is about 1 part bread to 3 parts tomato by weight.
Soak the torn bread in the tomatoes for 5 minutes. This softens the bread and makes it easier to blend.
Transfer everything to a blender: tomatoes, bread, garlic, olive oil, vinegar, and salt. Blend on high speed for 2 minutes until very smooth and creamy. Taste and add more vinegar or salt as needed.
Transfer to a covered bowl and refrigerate for at least 2 hours. The salmorejo thickens slightly as it chills.
Serve cold in bowls. Top each portion with chopped hard-boiled egg, torn serrano ham, and a drizzle of olive oil.

items.cause: Not enough bread for the tomato volume
items.fix: Add another slice of soaked bread and blend again. The consistency should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.

items.symptom: The salmorejo is too thick to pour
items.cause: Too much bread or too little oil
items.fix: Blend in a little cold water a tablespoon at a time until it flows. The oil also contributes to the creaminess.

items.cause: Garlic too raw for the volume, or tomatoes not ripe enough
items.fix: Use only 1 clove of garlic for a milder result. Ripe, sweet tomatoes are the base of a good salmorejo; unripe tomatoes will taste sharp regardless of the vinegar balance.

content.text: Salmorejo is the signature cold dish of Córdoba in Andalusia. It is closely related to gazpacho but distinct in its richness and density. In summer it is eaten daily in Córdoba as a starter, particularly at lunch. Outside Spain it is less well known than gazpacho but increasingly found on tapas menus.
